title: Hub connector for panels of a modular building structure
abstract: The invention relates to a hub connector (10) for a modular building structure (200) comprising a plurality of panels (110a, 110b, 110c, 110d, 110e, 110f) mounted on a framework structure (150). The hub connector (10) includes a supporting unit (14) comprising: rod connecting members (16) adapted for mechanically connecting framework rods (118) of the framework structure (150) to the supporting unit (14); electrical modules (20) configured to be electrically connected to an electrical panel connector (80) of corresponding panels of the plurality of panels (110a, 110b, 110c, 110d, 110e, 110f) of the modular building structure (200), and an electrical circuit (38) for electrically connecting together the electrical modules (20. The electrical circuit (38) comprises a conducting path surrounding the rod connecting members (16) through 360Â°. The invention also relates to a modular building structure (200) comprising a plurality of hub connectors (10) interconnecting framework rods (118) through their respective rod connecting members (16) to form a framework structure (150). The modular building structure (200) further comprises a plurality of panels (110a, 110b, 110c, 110d, 110e, 110f) connected to corresponding hub connectors (10).
claims:
Claims 1. Hub connector (10) for a modular building structure (200) comprising a plurality of panels (110a, 110b, 110c, 110d, 110e, 11 Of) mounted on a framework structure (150), the hub connector (10) having a supporting unit (14) comprising: - rod connecting members (16) adapted for mechanically connecting framework rods (118) of the framework structure (150) to the supporting unit (14), - electrical modules (20) configured to be electrically connected to an electrical panel connector (80) of corresponding panels of said plurality of panels (110a, 110b, 110c, 110d, 110e, 110f), and - an electrical circuit (38) for electrically connecting together said electrical modules (20), wherein the electrical circuit (38) comprises a conducting path surrounding the rod connecting members (16) through 360Â°. 2. Hub connector (10) according to claim 1, wherein said supporting unit (14) comprises at least three rod connecting members (16). 3. Hub connector (10) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the supporting unit (14) comprises panel connector anchoring portions (32, 34) configured to hold a panel connector (80, 96) of corresponding panels (110a, 110b, 110c, 110d, 110e, 110f) of the modular building structure (200). 4. Hub connector (10) according to any of claims 1 to 3, wherein the hub connector (10) further comprises an axially actuable unit (60) mounted in the supporting unit (14) and comprising a platform (66), said actuable unit (60) being configured to move linearly in an axial direction between a retracted position and an extended position. 5. Hub connector (10) according to claim 4, wherein each of said plurality of panels (110a, 110b, 110c, 110d, 110e, 11 Of) comprises an upper pane (112), an intermediate pane (114) and a lower pane (116), the supporting unit (14) of the hub connector comprising: a first set of panel connector anchoring portions (32) configured to hold panel connectors (96) of the upper panes (112) of corresponding panels (110a, 110b, 110c, 110d, 110e, 11 Of); a second set of panel connector anchoring portions (34) configured to hold panel connectors (80) of the intermediate panes (114) of said corresponding panels (110a, 110b, 110c, 110d, 110e, 110f), and wherein the platform (66) of the axially actuable unit (60) comprises a third set of panel connector anchoring portions (68) configured to hold panel connectors (98) of the lower panes (116) of said corresponding panels (110a, 110b, 110c, 110d, 110e, 110f). 6. Hub connector (10) according to claim 4 or 5, wherein the axially actuable unit (60) comprises: a shaft (62) extending upwardly from the centre of the platform (66), elongated locking members (65) disposed radially with respect to the shaft (62) and extending upwardly from said platform (66), and a compression spring (64) mounted around the shaft (62), the supporting unit (14) comprising a through-hole (44) and peripherical elongated cavities (54) extending from an upper part to a lower part of the supporting unit (14), said through-hole (44) and peripherical elongated cavities (54) being arranged to cooperate with the shaft (62) and the elongated locking members (65) respectively. 7. Hub connector (10) according to the preceding claim, further comprising an upper central locking portion (46) comprising locking 

surfaces (48), each elongated locking member (65) comprising a distal locking portion (65a) configured to cooperate with a respective locking surface (48), wherein the axially actuable unit (60) is brought to its extended position by the thrust applied by the compression spring (64) on the platform when said platform (66) is rotated with respect to the supporting unit (14) to disengage the distal locking portion (65a) of respective elongated locking member (65) from the corresponding locking surface (48). 8. Hub connector (10) according to any preceding claim, wherein the supporting unit (14) further comprising electrical module lodgings (30) inside which are mounted the electrical modules (20) electrically connected to the conducting path of the electrical circuit (38), the electrical modules (20) being preferably regularly spaced apart from each other through 360Â°. 9. Hub connector (10) according to the preceding claim, wherein the electrical circuit (38) is a Printed Circuit Board (PCB) extending through 360Â° around a central portion (46) of the supporting unit (14), the PCB (38) comprising connecting portions (40) regularly spaced apart from each other through 360Â° in correspondence with the position of the electrical modules (20) for electrically connecting each of said modules (20) to the PCB. 10. Hub connector (10) according to the preceding claim, wherein the PCB comprises a plurality of concentric electrical tracks, each of said modules (20) comprising a pin connector (26) having a plurality of pins, wherein each corresponding pin of the pin connector (26) of each module (20) are connected to the same electrical track. 11. Modular building structure (200) comprising a plurality of hub connectors (10) according to any preceding claim, wherein the hub connectors (10) interconnect framework rods (118) through their respective rod connecting members (16) to form a framework structure (150), the modular building structure (200) further comprising a plurality of panels (110a, 110b, 110c, 110d, 110e, 110f ) connected to corresponding hub connectors (10). 12. Modular building structure (200) according to the preceding claim, wherein each panel of said plurality of panels (110a, 110b, 110c, 110d, 110e, 110f ) comprises an upper pane (112), an intermediate pane (114) and a lower pane (116), wherein at least one of the upper pane (112), intermediate pane (114) and lower pane (116) of each panel comprises an electrical panel connector (80) electrically connected to a corresponding electrical module (20) of a corresponding hub connector (10). 13. Modular building structure (200) according to claim 12, wherein at least one of the upper, intermediate and lower panes (112, 114, 116) of each of said plurality of panels (110a, 110b, 110c, 110d, 110e, 110f) are transparent, the upper and lower panes (112, 116) being preferably transparent and the intermediate pane preferably comprising said electrical panel connector (80). 14. Modular building structure (200) according to claim 12 or 13, wherein at least one of the upper, intermediate and lower panes (112, 114, 116) of each of said plurality of panels (110a, 110b, 110c, 110d, 110e, 110f) is a smart glass configured to change from transparent to translucent or opaque and vice versa, the intermediate pane (114) being preferably the only smart glass among the upper, intermediate and lower panes (112, 114, 116). 15. Modular building structure (200) according to any of claims 12 to 14, further comprising at least one power source (130) and a control unit (140) electrically connected to each of at least one of the upper, intermediate and lower panes (112, 114, 116) of said plurality of panels (110a, 110b, 110c, 110d, 110e, 110f), wherein the control unit (140) is configured to send a control signal to any of said at least one of the upper, intermediate and lower panes (112, 114, 116) of said plurality of panels to power a selected number of upper, intermediate or lower panes as a function of the respective position of the plurality of panels (110a, 110b, 110c, 110d, 110e, 11 Of). 16. Modular building structure (200) according to the preceding claim, wherein each of at least one of the upper, intermediate and lower pane (112, 114, 116) of said plurality of panels is a smart glass configured to change from transparent to translucent or opaque and vice versa, the control unit (140) comprising a processor configured to execute a program to control the appearance of each smart glass (114) as a function of the position of the sun.
